goal: to be able to easily sync my app data across multiple devices
benefit: I can access my information from anywhere
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Sync 5 text notes across iOS and Web within 5 seconds. 2. Edit offline on Device A, reconnect, verify cloud update. 3. Cause simultaneous edit conflict and check resolution prompt.
notes: Annotators agreed it's a solid story (score 5). Multi-device sync is a clear, valuable user goal. Could use a bit more detail on what data specifically is synced.

goal: to be able to check transaction history and keep a record of it
benefit: I can go back when needed
expected_test_scenarios: View transaction history with list of past transactions; Filter by date or type; Export or download record; Empty state when no transactions exist
notes: Goal 'check transaction history and keep a record' is vague — no format, filter, or time range specified. Benefit is weak ('when needed'). Testable in basic form but edge cases unclear.

goal: to control all of my smart devices from a single app, so I can easily manage my home's technology
benefit: I can easily manage my home's technology
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Pair a new smart bulb via app. 2. Group 3 devices and turn all off with one tap. 3. Display 'Device Offline' icon when router is disconnected.
notes: Good clarity on the smart home domain. A2 flagged that 'all of my smart devices' might be too broad (scope creep risk). Resolved to 4.

goal: to know all the collections and objects in the DAMS for which I have custodial responsibility
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Login as repo manager and view dashboard of assigned collections. 2. Attempt to access unassigned collection via direct URL (expect 403). 3. Filter objects by active status.
notes: Role 'repository manager' is well-defined. Goal is slightly passive ('to know'). A1 suggested refactoring to 'view a list of...'.

goal: to be able to customize the layout of my dashboard so that I can arrange the information in a way that suits me
benefit: I can arrange the information in a way that suits me
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Drag 'Activity' widget to top left. 2. Resize 'Charts' widget to 2x2. 3. Logout/login and verify layout persists.
notes: Classic layout customization story. Both agreed it's well-formed. Missing specific constraints on what 'layout' entails, but valid at epic/high-level.

goal: an app that generates an OpenRefine reconciliation API endpoint from a Tabular Data Package
benefit: I can use it to clean messy data
expected_test_scenarios: App generates valid OpenRefine endpoint from a data package; Invalid data package returns error; Endpoint accessible and returns expected format; Messy data can be cleaned via the endpoint
notes: Highly technical story; role 'Researcher' vague — not a system actor. Benefit is measurable ('clean messy data') but goal is too implementation-specific for a user story. Annotator 2 flagged missing acceptance criteria scope.

goal: an DSpace integration
benefit: I can integrate data-packaged data with pipelines that use Dspace
expected_test_scenarios: DSpace integration connects successfully; Data package imported via DSpace pipeline; Integration fails gracefully with error message; Data integrity preserved after import
notes: Goal 'an DSpace integration' is a feature request, not a user goal — grammatically incorrect ('an DSpace') and too broad. Benefit is minimal ('integrate with pipelines'). Low testability without sub-stories.

goal: to be able to browse through the list of facolities and see which ones are environment-friendly
benefit: I can know for sure my waste is not going to leave a negative ecological footprint
expected_test_scenarios: Browse facility list with ecological rating visible; Filter facilities by ecological impact; Facility detail shows waste disposal method; User can confirm facility meets environmental standard
notes: Meaningful civic story; typo ('facolities') noted. Benefit about ecological impact is clear and testable. Annotator 2 noted goal is compound — 'browse AND see' should be split.

goal: to link datasets with the project DMP
benefit: compliance with DMP can be demonstrated and whole project workflow is linked together
expected_test_scenarios: Dataset links to DMP successfully; Link visible in both dataset and DMP records; Unlinking removes the association; DMP compliance status updated after link
notes: Clear data management story; compliance angle makes benefit concrete. Linkage and unlinkage both testable. Domain (research data) may require specialist knowledge to evaluate fully.

goal: to be able to track the status of a loan application, so I can manage my work effectively
benefit: I can manage my work effectively
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Enter valid application ID and see 'In Progress' status. 2. Verify status updates to 'Approved' in real-time when backend changes. 3. Display submission date.
notes: Missing explicit benefit originally. Disagreement stems from whether 'manage my work' is a strong enough business value. Resolved based on A1's stricter criteria.

goal: to migrate data from an older version of NeuroHub to the current version
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Run migration script on v1.0 DB. 2. Check logs for 0 missing records in v2.0 DB. 3. Verify specific edge case: old null fields map to default values in current version.
notes: A1 flagged this as an engineering task (migration), not a user story. A2 agreed it lacks a true end-user benefit. Scored low.

goal: to specify schema as a SQL schema string
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Input 'CREATE TABLE x (id INT)' and verify schema parsing. 2. Input invalid SQL 'CREATE TBL' and return syntax error line. 3. Reject SQL injection commands (DROP, DELETE).
notes: Extremely technical. 'Specify schema as SQL string' is an implementation detail, not a user need. Both annotators penalized this heavily.

goal: to contact the author of a work that interests me
benefit: I can learn more about my research
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Click 'Contact Author' on a paper page. 2. Fill 500-char message and submit. 3. Verify recipient gets email with correct reply-to headers.
notes: Clear, concise. 'Contact the author' is a standard feature. A2 noted that it doesn't specify the communication channel (email vs in-app).

goal: to upload and tag staff generated working papers
benefit: staff and researchers are able to easily access them
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Upload 10MB PDF working paper. 2. Tag with '2026', 'Finance'. 3. Search 'Finance' and verify document is top result.
notes: Slight disagreement on role clarity ('staff generated'). Resolved to higher score as the business value is very clear to researchers.

goal: to Complete Building Development Project
benefit: I can receive any posted bond and/or obtain occupancy
expected_test_scenarios: Application form submission triggers project completion step; System validates required fields before completion; Bond released on successful completion; Occupancy permit issued after approval
notes: Goal 'Complete Building Development Project' is extremely vague — could mean dozens of sub-tasks. Benefit (bond + occupancy) is concrete but story is too high-level to generate focused acceptance tests. Annotator 2 marked as needs decomposition.

goal: to have the training options clearly marked for the following information: day and time of training, title, short summary, trainers, level, number of registrations, remaining registrations, location of training, link to full details of the training, register now link
benefit: I can shop for training all with one page AND find their class on on one page
expected_test_scenarios: Training page shows all options (name, cost, dates, location, skill level, prerequisites); All classes on one page with filtering; Clicking class links to detailed class page; Page loads correctly without sign-in
notes: Good story with multiple concrete data fields in goal. Benefit (shop + find on one page) is dual but both are measurable. Some ambiguity around 'clearly marked' — needs definition of done.

goal: an option to stay logged in
benefit: I don't have to enter my credentials every time
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Check 'Stay logged in', close browser, reopen, verify session active. 2. Force session expiry on backend, verify next page load redirects to login.
notes: Very simple utility story. 'Option to stay logged in'. Annotators gave mid-range scores due to lack of specificity on security constraints (timeout period).

goal: to accurately see who created a submission
benefit: I'm not confused about who last updated a submission
expected_test_scenarios: Submission creator name is displayed correctly; Last updated user is shown distinctly from creator; Submission history shows both create and update events; No confusion when same user creates and updates
notes: Simple, clear story about data provenance. Benefit ('not confused') is business-meaningful and testable. Edge case: same user creates and updates — needs explicit test.

goal: to create and edit a Resource record
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Click 'New Resource', fill all mandatory fields, hit Save. 2. Edit title of existing Resource. 3. Leave 'Resource Type' blank and check inline validation error.
notes: Missing benefit entirely. 'Create and edit a Resource record' is standard CRUD. Disagreement resolved to lower score due to missing 'Why'.

goal: to create a RESTful API so that our front end can communicate with our back end
benefit: our front end can communicate with our back end
expected_test_scenarios: 1. GET /api/v1/users returns 200 OK and valid JSON. 2. POST /api/v1/users with missing payload returns 400 Bad Request. 3. Verify JWT token authentication works on all endpoints.
notes: This is an architectural task ('create a RESTful API'), not a user story. Both annotators heavily penalized it. Needs to be rewritten from frontend user perspective.

goal: to track my order, so I can know when to expect delivery
benefit: I can know when to expect delivery
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Enter order #12345, see timeline UI (Ordered -> Shipped -> Delivered). 2. Enter non-existent order #999, see 'Order not found' graphic. 3. Display estimated delivery date.
notes: Standard e-commerce story. 'Track my order' is clear. Acceptance criteria would need to define what statuses exist (Shipped, Transit, etc).

goal: to transfer funds between my accounts, so I can manage my money effectively
benefit: I can manage my money effectively
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Transfer $50 from Checking to Savings, verify balances instantly update. 2. Attempt to transfer more than available balance (expect error). 3. Verify transaction appears in history.
notes: Banking domain. Role is just 'user' (implicit). Story is fine but basic. A2 wanted more specifics on 'effectively' managing money.

goal: to choose my own username and password during registration
benefit: I can personalize my login credentials
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Register with username 'JohnDoe' and strong password. 2. Attempt to register 'JohnDoe' again (expect duplicate error). 3. Check password strength meter turns green.
notes: Good registration story. Disagreement because A1 felt 'personalize login' is a weak benefit statement compared to actual security needs.

goal: to refactor our legacy code so that it's easier to maintain and extend
benefit: it's easier to maintain and extend
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Run unit test suite post-refactor; expect 100% pass rate. 2. Run SonarQube; verify cyclomatic complexity reduced by 10%. 3. Smoke test core UI to ensure no regressions.
notes: 'Refactor our legacy code' - classic anti-pattern for user stories. This is a technical chore. Both annotators correctly scored this low.

goal: to be quickly log attendance everyday
benefit: I can track which kids are coming in
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Open class list, tap 'Present All'. 2. Manually toggle one student to 'Absent'. 3. Submit day's attendance and verify database timestamp.
notes: Educational/School domain. 'Quickly log attendance'. Typo in original ('to be quickly log'). Annotators agreed it's understandable but poorly phrased.

goal: to know when Zoonibot should comment on a subject
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Trigger specific workflow rule; verify Zoonibot posts comment within 2 seconds. 2. Check Zoonibot comment contains correct markdown formatting.
notes: Zoonibot story. Lacks benefit. 'To know when Zoonibot should comment' is vague. Score reflects the ambiguity of the trigger conditions.

goal: to change my account details
benefit: I can keep my account details up-to-date
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Navigate to Settings, change phone number. 2. Verify OTP is sent to new phone. 3. Old phone number cannot be used to log in.
notes: CRUD operation. Benefit is a bit tautological ('keep details up-to-date'). Needs more specific value proposition for the user.

goal: to see product recommendations on my dashboard
benefit: I can discover new products I might like
expected_test_scenarios: 1. View dashboard as returning user; see 5 recommended items based on cart history. 2. View as new user; see 'Trending Now' default items. 3. Click recommendation, verify it opens product page.
notes: Clear e-commerce recommendation story. Both agreed on high score. Suggestion: specify if recommendations are based on past purchases or browsing.

goal: to reserve a table online, so I can ensure I have a place to eat at my preferred time
benefit: I can ensure I have a place to eat at my preferred time
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Pick June 15, 7 PM, Party of 4; verify booking confirmed. 2. Pick fully booked time; verify slot is disabled in UI. 3. Receive email confirmation.
notes: Restaurant booking. Well structured with 'so I can'. Disagreement from A2 who wanted to know if 'preferred time' implies a calendar view UI.

goal: to add a date delimiter to a search string
benefit: I can limit a search to return only results from before, after, or between a given date or dates
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Search with delimiter 'after:2025-01-01'; verify no 2024 results. 2. Search 'between:2025-01-01,2025-12-31'; check boundaries. 3. Input malformed date '2025-13-45'; see format error.
notes: Search feature. Benefit is highly specific and clear. Annotators loved the detail on 'before, after, or between'. Scored very well.

goal: to create custom workflows in Jira so that our process accurately reflects in the tool
benefit: our process accurately reflects in the tool
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Create custom transition 'To QA Review'. 2. Set condition 'Only QA Lead can transition'. 3. Test as Dev (fails), test as QA Lead (succeeds).
notes: Jira workflow customization. A1 flagged that 'accurately reflects in the tool' is a bit subjective, leading to a minor disagreement.

goal: to give corrective feedback if exercises are performed wrong, too much or in any other way harmful to the user
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Perform squat with knees past toes; verify visual 'Corrective' prompt on screen. 2. Perform 50 reps quickly; verify 'Rest' warning triggers. 3. Verify audio feedback option can be muted.
notes: Fitness app domain. 'Give corrective feedback' is complex but clear. A2 noted that 'in any other way harmful' is too broad for QA to test.

goal: to know the security requirements of the data
benefit: I can check whether the data can be kept secure
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Open data asset; verify security badge ('Confidential') is visible. 2. Click badge to view detailed compliance requirements. 3. As guest user, verify highly secure assets are hidden.
notes: Security domain. 'Know the security requirements'. A1 found it passive. Needs an action-oriented goal (e.g., 'View security badge').

goal: to implement a caching mechanism so that we can reduce page load times
benefit: we can reduce page load times
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Load homepage (cold cache) - measure TTFB. 2. Reload homepage (warm cache) - verify TTFB < 200ms. 3. Update DB record; verify cache invalidates and UI shows new data.
notes: Performance task ('implement caching mechanism'). Annotators penalized it as it's an implementation detail, not a user-facing capability.

goal: to copy one of my courses or events
benefit: I can create a new one
expected_test_scenarios: Copied course retains all fields (name, date, description, fee); Copied event appears as a new draft, not published; Original course/event unchanged after copy; Trainer can edit the copy independently
notes: Concise, unambiguous CRUD copy story. Role (trainer), goal (copy), benefit (create new) all clear. Positive and negative paths easy to derive. High testability.

goal: to learn about how Github is used
benefit: I can participate in the issues as assigned
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Trigger 'First time user' tour on issue board. 2. Click 'Next' through all 4 steps. 3. Verify 'Help' icon links to official GitHub docs.
notes: GitHub usage. 'Learn about how Github is used' is not a system feature, it's a documentation need. Both scored poorly.

goal: to permanently delete a data package
benefit: that it no longer takes up storage space
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Click 'Delete Package'; see modal 'Type DELETE to confirm'. 2. Confirm; verify package vanishes from dashboard. 3. Check AWS S3 bucket to ensure actual objects are purged.
notes: Data management. 'Permanently delete'. A1 noted it needs mention of cascading deletes or warnings. Good story otherwise.

goal: to categorize my transactions, so I can better understand and manage my spending habits
benefit: I can better understand and manage my spending habits
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Click unassigned transaction, select 'Groceries' category. 2. Setup rule: 'All Walmart = Groceries'; verify past transactions update. 3. View pie chart with updated category totals.
notes: Banking/PFM. 'Categorize transactions'. Very clear 'so I can' clause. Both annotators agreed it's a solid, testable story.

goal: to compose collections, limiting the collection to the items sharing the same provenance, limiting the collection to represent a part of a collection have a singular provenance, or assembly a collection from other collections and objects
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Select 5 items with provenance 'Batch A'; group into 'Collection A'. 2. Select 'Collection A' and 'Item B'; form 'Collection C' (nested). 3. Verify hierarchy renders correctly in tree view.
notes: Archive domain. Extremely verbose and complex goal ('compose collections, limiting...'). A1 penalized due to unreadable structure.

goal: to deposit arbitrarily large files
benefit: I am not limited in what files I can and cannot deposit
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Initiate 50GB file upload via web UI. 2. Disconnect WiFi at 50%, reconnect, verify upload resumes. 3. Post-upload, verify file checksum matches local file.
notes: Archive domain. 'Arbitrarily large files' is a testing nightmare. A2 flagged this for scope definition, resulting in a mid-tier score.

goal: to see textual descriptions that accompany embedded visualisations
benefit: I can more easily understand what I am viewing
expected_test_scenarios: Visualisation displayed with accompanying text description; Description appears adjacent to or below the chart; Description updates when visualisation data changes; Page renders correctly when no description is provided
notes: Clear accessibility/comprehension story. Benefit ('understand what I am viewing') is concrete. Annotator 2 noted 'textual descriptions' could mean tooltip, caption, or alt-text — ambiguity in implementation spec.

goal: to track use of a photo
benefit: I know if I've sent it to a researcher before
expected_test_scenarios: 1. View Photo Details; see 'Downloads: 14'. 2. Click count to open log; view timestamps and User IDs. 3. Export usage log to CSV.
notes: Photo management. Benefit 'I know if I've sent it' is very practical. Both annotators gave high marks. Good real-world context.

goal: to Submit Supporting Documentation
benefit: I can satisfy documentation requirements for my application
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Upload 3 PDFs (Tax Form, ID, Statement) simultaneously. 2. Attempt to upload .exe file (rejected). 3. Submit application, verify all 3 docs are attached to reviewer payload.
notes: Application portal. 'Submit Supporting Documentation'. Clear goal, but role is generic. Solid story overall.

goal: to view and download summary embargo metadata for all ETDs in the repository
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Click 'Export Embargoes'. 2. Open resulting CSV; verify columns: Title, Author, Embargo End Date. 3. Log in as student; verify export button is completely hidden.
notes: Missing benefit. 'View and download summary embargo metadata'. Highly specific to ETDs, but format is incomplete without the 'so that'.

goal: to have the ability to attach detailed meta data to the data and entries that I create
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Open entry #102; add custom field key='Source', value='Field Survey'. 2. Edit 'Source' to 'Lab Test'. 3. API query filtering by metadata key 'Source' returns correct entry.
notes: Data entry. 'Attach detailed meta data'. A1 flagged 'detailed' as ambiguous. Resolved to lower score to encourage clarity.

goal: to have ALFRED to offer training for specific muscle groups
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Select 'Chest' from muscle group filter; see only bench press/pushup plans. 2. Verify ALFRED system generates dynamic 4-week calendar for selected group. 3. Check progress bar updates per muscle.
notes: Fitness domain. 'ALFRED to offer training'. 'ALFRED' is the system, not the user. Annotators penalized because it's written from system's perspective.

goal: to access the raw agency published files from FABS via USAspending
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Access USAspending portal, locate FABS dataset. 2. Click 'Download Raw XML'; verify file size is non-zero. 3. Verify XML schema matches federal standard.
notes: Data portal. 'Access raw agency published files'. Missing benefit. Straightforward but incomplete format.

goal: to install Neurohub dependencies into an Ubuntu 10.04 LTS machine using the normal Ubuntu package management tools
benefit: Benefit not explicitly stated.
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Run 'sudo apt-get install neurohub-deps' on clean Ubuntu 10.04 VM. 2. Run 'neurohub --version' to confirm successful launch. 3. Run on Ubuntu 22.04; verify graceful OS unsupported error.
notes: DevOps task ('install Neurohub dependencies into Ubuntu 10.04'). Complete anti-pattern. Both annotators rejected it as a user story.

goal: to be charged a fee once I am approved for Practitioner status
benefit: my certification status can be activated
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Admin sets user to 'Approved'; user receives payment link. 2. User pays $50 via Stripe; status auto-changes to 'Practitioner'. 3. Test card declined scenario; status remains 'Pending Payment'.
notes: Certification domain. 'Be charged a fee'. A bit weird to have a goal to be charged, but business logic makes sense. Rated well.

goal: to have private data packages
benefit: I can share just with my team
expected_test_scenarios: 1. Create package 'Q1 Data'; set to 'Private'. 2. Share via email with teammate; teammate can access. 3. Share URL with external account; verify 403 Access Denied.
notes: Data sharing. 'Have private data packages'. Clear benefit ('share just with my team'). Annotators agreed on high value.
